title: elytrigia repens subsp. koeleri
intro: Elytrigia repens subsp. koeleri, also known as Koeleri's couch grass or Elymus koeleri, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of western North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Elytrigia repens subsp. koeleri include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an expectorant to ease respiratory congestion. Elytrigia repens subsp. koeleri can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
